# Glossary {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note}
This plain-language glossary is being expanded (an accessibility
and ESL-support goal of the project). Terms are defined on first
use within each chapter; this page collects the most common ones
in one place.
:::

**Tibble** — a modern kind of data frame (table) used by the
tidyverse. Each column is a variable; each row is an
observation.

**Pipe (`|>`)** — the native R operator that passes the result
on its left into the function on its right, so a workflow reads
left to right, top to bottom.

**Data dictionary** — a short document, one row per column, that
records each variable's name, meaning, units, and type.

**Cleaning log** — a transparent record of every change made to
raw data on the way to an analysis-ready dataset.

**CRS (coordinate reference system)** — the system that ties
spatial coordinates to locations on Earth (e.g., WGS 84, BC
Albers). Reprojecting converts data from one CRS to another.

**Quarto** — the open-source publishing system that combines
prose, code, and output into a single rendered report
(HTML, PDF, Word, slides).

**Open Government Licence (OGL)** — the licence under which BC,
Ontario, and federal open data are released, permitting reuse
with attribution.
